div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church both future and present, Richard de Morville, constable of the king of the Scots, sends greeting. Know me to have given, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and the monks serving God in that place, in free and quit and everlasting alms, the freedom and licence to have at Buckholm one cow pasture for sixty cows, and a suitable building for their needs, within the same enclosure which was there before I had given them this permission. Wherefore I will that the aforenamed church of St Mary at Melrose and the foresaid monks should have the forementioned liberty and licence in free and everlasting alms, and possess it for ever; as freely, quitely, and honourably as they possess any alms freely, quitely, and honourably; so that it shall non remain a hindrance on account of my charter for Whitelee which I had given to them previously, which shows me to have granted to them that they may have only three buildings in the forest, and that at the foresaid Whitelee. As witness: the lady Avice of Lancaster; William de Morville our son and heir; Herbert the dean of Glasgow; Master Clement, chaplain; Adam, parson of the church of Lauder; Roger clerk and steward; Alan of Thirlestane; Sampson son of William of Oxton.
